mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0% for at least three consecutive months, the cumulative drag of restrictive real rates on corporate financing costs and equity valuations produces below-average S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter, as discount-rate pressure outweighs any soft-landing optimism.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of 2023-10-01 vintage, highest since 2001
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS>5.0% regimes are positive in more than 55% of instances or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A stable or declining UNRATE reading below its trailing 12-month average signals labor market resilience that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of other macro conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9% as of 2023-10-01, near multi-decade lows
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns following low/falling UNRATE readings are not statistically distinguishable from the unconditional 65.5% positive base rate (chi-square p > 0.10), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in RSAFS growth and a decline in HOUST below its 6-month moving average jointly signal a broad-based consumer and housing demand slowdown that forward returns fail to price in, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month; neither series alone reliably predicts this outcome.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth < 0 AND HOUST below 6-month moving average simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised value 686148 vs vintage 704954 showing downward revision
fact:HOUST at 1371 thousand units as of 2023-09-01
failure_condition: If joint-trigger episodes produce SPX 20-day forward returns that are not significantly more negative than single-series-triggered episodes (t-test p > 0.05), the interaction hypothesis is rejected in favor of independent effects.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a sustained three-month decline in INDPRO signals weakening industrial output that precedes broader earnings deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; this relationship is muted or absent during early-cycle recoveries when inventory rebuilding dominates.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised value 101.0211 down from vintage 103.6115, indicating downward revision and weakening trend
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day forward returns following 3-month INDPRO declines during late-cycle windows are positive in more than 55% of instances, or if the effect is equally strong in early-cycle windows, the regime-conditional hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A month where CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation decelerates while FEDFUNDS remains unchanged signals that real policy rates are rising passively, creating short-term equity market uncertainty about the Fed's next move; this joint condition produces elevated volatility and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week, an effect not captured by either series alone.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth decelerates by >0.2pp month-over-month while FEDFUNDS is unchanged
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 307.696 revised vs 307.619 vintage, FEDFUNDS unchanged at 5.33%
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns during joint disinflation-plus-unchanged-rate episodes are not significantly more negative than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or if single-series CPIAUCSL deceleration alone explains the same effect, the interaction hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS month-over-month net job gains fall below 100,000 after a period of stronger gains, this threshold crossing signals an inflection toward labor market cooling that increases recession risk pricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM change falls below 100,000 after prior 3-month average above 150,000
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised value 156576 thousand down from vintage 156923, indicating downward payroll revision
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day forward returns following the PAYEMS threshold crossing are positive in more than 55% of instances, the threshold hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained uptrend in HOUST above its trailing 12-month average signals durable housing demand and consumer confidence that feeds into broader economic growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter across all macro regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1371 thousand units, revised up from vintage 1358 as of 2023-09-01
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns during HOUST uptrend periods are not significantly higher than the unconditional base rate (t-test p > 0.05) across at least two distinct 3-year windows, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary policy pause regime, defined by FEDFUNDS remaining unchanged for at least three consecutive months at a cycle-high level, equity markets front-run an anticipated pivot to easing, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week; this effect reverses if the pause regime instead precedes renewed hiking.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS unchanged for 3+ consecutive months at or near cycle high
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS unchanged at 5.33% since mid-2023, held at cycle peak
regime_hint: monetary policy pause at cycle peak
failure_condition: If SPX 5-day forward returns during confirmed pause regimes are negative in more than 50% of instances, or if the effect reverses sign once a subsequent hike occurs within the same pause window, the regime-conditional hypothesis is rejected.
